description: Wan2.1 VACE 14B video inpainting / object removal. A masked region is regenerated from the text prompt + surrounding video (no reference image = removal, not replacement). Graph: ComfyUI core template 'video_wan_vace_inpainting', reference_image disconnected. Accelerated with the CausVid LoRA (4 steps, cfg 1).
_HOWITRUNS: No-build path: a public ComfyUI base image runs comfy_run.py, which seeds ComfyUI from the image bundle, downloads the 4 weights + CausVid LoRA onto the Spark node, converts the chosen workflow (UI graph) to API format via /object_info, applies the patches, and renders. All node types are CORE ComfyUI (no node_packs) — but they are RECENT core nodes (WanVaceToVideo, LoadVideo, CreateVideo), so the base image must ship a current ComfyUI; the cu130 yanwk tag does. ~42 GB is fetched on each COLD start (use --idle-hold to keep a warm node). --convert-only skips the model download.
_MASKMODES: Mask supports BOTH a static image and a per-frame mask video, auto-selected by --mask extension. A video extension (.mp4/.mov/.webm/...) switches to 'workflow_mask_video' and remaps the mask param to the LoadVideo 'file' input (mask_video_param). White = the region to inpaint/remove; the mask should match the source video's frame count and resolution.
_PARAMS: Node ids come from the UI graph and survive conversion; patches target API format post-conversion. If a patch warns 'not in graph' or output looks wrong, pull the converted workflow_api.json (written to ShareSync each run) and check field names. KSampler is preset for CausVid (4 steps, cfg 1, uni_pc/simple); for the base model without CausVid use steps ~20, cfg ~6 (--set 3.inputs.steps=20 --set 3.inputs.cfg=6 and --strength 0).

ui.title: Wan2.1 VACE — masked video inpainting / object removal

primary_input.label: Input video
primary_input.tooltip: The source clip. The masked region (white in the mask) is regenerated from the prompt + surrounding video.

prompt.default: an empty cobblestone street, clean background, consistent lighting and perspective

ui.label: Prompt
ui.tooltip: For REMOVAL, describe the CLEAN result that should fill the masked area — naming the surrounding scene/surface ('empty cobblestone street') works better than 'background, empty'. For REPLACEMENT, describe the new object. (src: Wan VACE community guidance / ComfyUI docs)

negative.default: the removed object, people, blur, ghosting, warping, flicker, seams, artifacts

ui.label: Negative prompt
ui.tooltip: Discourage re-introducing the removed content and common inpaint artifacts. Wan's official negative is a long Chinese quality string; this English one targets clean removal.

ui.label: CausVid strength
ui.tooltip: CausVid acceleration LoRA strength (4-step, cfg 1). Try 0.3–0.7. Set 0 to run the base model instead — then bump steps ~20 / cfg ~6 via --set.
